cards.front: What is the Open Handset Alliance?
cards.back: A consortium of companies that develops and maintains the Android operating system

cards.front: What is the difference between Android versions?
cards.back: Each version is a major update that includes new features, security patches, and performance improvements
A single screen in an Android application that can perform specific tasks or display information

cards.front: What are some key features of Android?
cards.back: These include a user interface, apps, widgets, notifications, multitasking, and hardware customization

cards.front: What is the Android Architecture Stack?
cards.back: A design pattern that separates an application into layers, including the presentation, business logic, and data storage layers

cards.front: What is the Linux Kernel in relation to Android?
cards.back: A modified version of the Linux kernel is used as the basis for the Android operating system
